Container.Name: database-backup

Branch.TagDescription: Latest stable release, v2 (multi-arch: amd64, arm64). Pin tag 1 to stay on the legacy bash version.

Container.Overview:
Lightweight Go-powered container to automate PostgreSQL, MariaDB/MySQL, and MongoDB backups on a schedule.
Features: - Simple YAML configuration with strict validation and a built-in validate command - Dynamic client install at startup: only the tools your config needs are installed - Flexible cron scheduling per backup job, with graceful shutdown for in-flight backups - Streaming gzip compression, atomic file writes, timestamped dumps - Optional at-rest encryption: gpg-compatible OpenPGP or age (passphrase or public key) - Configurable per-job retention; old backups are only rotated after a successful run - Runs unprivileged (uid 1000) after client installation; secrets never appear in process arguments - Built-in healthcheck using native database drivers (no heavy CLI downloads) - Multiple databases (different types and hosts) handled by a single container - Multi-architecture (amd64, arm64) and auto-rebuilt on base image security updates
Setup: 1. Copy backups.yml.example from the project repository to your appdata directory and rename it to backups.yml 2. Edit backups.yml with your databases (chmod 600, it contains credentials) 3. Map the file to /config/backups.yml and a host folder to /backups
Upgrading from v1: run the built-in migrate command to convert your old backups.conf to YAML. See https://github.com/Greite/database-backup/blob/main/docs/MIGRATION-V2.md

Config.Name: Backups Storage
Config.Description: Host path where database dumps are written. Files are organized as <type>/<job-name>/<job-name>_<timestamp>.sql.gz (or .tar.gz for MongoDB), plus a .gpg or .age suffix when encryption is enabled.

Config.Name: Configuration File
Config.Description: Path to the backups.yml file on the host. Get the example from https://github.com/Greite/database-backup/blob/main/backups.yml.example and edit it before starting the container. Upgrading from v1? Convert your backups.conf with the built-in migrate command (see the migration guide linked in the Overview).

Config.Name: Encryption Passphrase
Config.Description: Optional. Enables gpg-compatible at-rest encryption of every backup (decrypt with gpg -d). Leave empty to disable, or use the encryption block in backups.yml for more options (age, key files).
